title: Password Spray Against the Domain
briefing: Domain host $TARGET is seeing 4625 logon failures spread across MANY different accounts from only one or two external sources — a small number of attempts each. This is the classic shape of a password spray (low-and-slow, one password vs many users) rather than a single-account brute force. Enumerate the sources, recognise the pattern, and scope the sprayed accounts.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.id: 100110. Group by data.win.eventdata.targetUserName — expect MANY distinct users, FEW attempts each, from the same 1-2 data.srcip values.

questions.prompt: What specifically distinguishes this from a normal brute force?
questions.explain: Spraying tries one/few passwords across many accounts to stay under lockout thresholds — broad targetUserName spread, low count per user.

options: Hunt for any SUCCESSFUL 4624 from those IPs
Force reset on any account that authenticated
Do nothing since no single account was locked
answer: Hunt for any SUCCESSFUL 4624 from those IPs
Force reset on any account that authenticated
questions.explain: Spraying often stays below lockout — the real risk is one quiet success. Block, hunt for 4624 from the same IPs, reset any hit, enforce MFA.
